summary: NullPointerException in getVideoStreamIndex when opening the video quality selector
description: Users report a hard crash the moment they tap the resolution/quality button on a video. The app dies with a java.lang.NullPointerException raised while ranking the available video streams: String.replaceAll is invoked on a null resolution value inside ListHelper.getVideoStreamIndex. It reproduces on certain streams whose extracted resolution string is missing (null) - the default-resolution lookup at ListHelper.getDefaultResolutionIndex walks every video stream and calls getResolution(), then immediately dereferences the result without a null guard. Attaching a logcat capture from a Pixel device; the fatal frame is ListHelper.java:590. Expected: a stream with a missing resolution should be skipped, not crash the player.

logs.content:
FATAL EXCEPTION: main
Process: com.example.app, PID: 12043
java.lang.NullPointerException: Attempt to invoke virtual method 'java.lang.String java.lang.String.replaceAll(java.lang.String, java.lang.String)' on a null object reference
	at com.example.app.util.ListHelper.getVideoStreamIndex(ListHelper.java:590)
	at com.example.app.util.ListHelper.getDefaultResolutionIndex(ListHelper.java:439)
	at com.example.app.util.ListHelper.getDefaultResolutionWithDefaultFormat(ListHelper.java:644)
	at com.example.app.util.ListHelper.getDefaultResolutionIndex(ListHelper.java:85)
	at com.example.app.player.VideoPlayerUi.buildQualityMenu(VideoPlayerUi.java:412)
	at com.example.app.player.Player.initPlayback(Player.java:731)
	at android.os.Handler.handleCallback(Handler.java:938)
	at android.os.Handler.dispatchMessage(Handler.java:99)
	at android.os.Looper.loop(Looper.java:223)
	at android.app.ActivityThread.main(ActivityThread.java:7656)
	at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Native Method)
	at com.android.internal.os.RuntimeInit$MethodAndArgsCaller.run(RuntimeInit.java:592)
	at com.android.internal.os.ZygoteInit.main(ZygoteInit.java:947)
